page_text_excerpt: Alumni Our fellowship alumni are spread across the country serving in a diverse array of positions and following career paths of their choice. They have become leaders in academic medicine as well as private practice cardiology, government, and industry. As a result of the unique, individualized training they received in our program, recent graduates have obtained positions such as: Medical Director, Heartbeat Health (telemedicine startup) Director, Advanced Congenital Cardiac Therapies, Vanderbilt University Director, Cardiac Intensive Care Unit, MaineHealth Associate Chief, Clinical Cardiology, Oregon Health and Science University Clinical Lead for Innovation, MaineHealth Chief Strategy Officer for Coverage and Analysis, Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services Chief Investigator, Section of Inflammation and Cardiometabolic Diseases, National Institutes of Health Assistant Chief Medical Information Officer, Connected Health, Penn Medicine Assistant Fellowship Training Program Director, Hospital of the University of Pennsylvania Email us at: [REDACTED_EMAIL] , or ask on your interview day to be connected to a member of our broad alumni network who has interests similar to yours!

profile_text_excerpt: Michael Song, MD Medical School: Rutgers New Jersey Medical School Residency Program: University of Michigan Program/Specialty: Clinical Scholar What inspires me about GI : The broad range of clinical conditions necessitating a blend of acute treatment and longitudinal follow up and the frequent opportunities to provide collaborative care via multidisciplinary teams My interests within GI : risk stratification in MASLD and cirrhosis Hobbies : traveling, weightlifting, sports, spending time with friends and family Why I chose Penn : The comprehensive clinical training, the renowned faculty, and Philadelphia

medical_school: University of South Florida
name: Saif Zaman, MD
profile_text_excerpt: Saif Zaman, MD Medical School: University of South Florida Residency Program: Yale University Program/Specialty: Clinical Epidemiology What inspires me about GI : The intricate and multifaceted nature of the gastrointestinal system. The ability to integrate cutting-edge research with clinical practice to address complex disorders and the ongoing advancements in endoscopic technology inspire me to be at the forefront of this dynamic field. My interests within GI : Include the molecular mechanisms of gut motility disorders, gastrointestinal bioelectromagnetics, enteric nervous system disorders, the role of bile acids in gut microbiome interactions, the application of artificial intelligence in endoscopy, the impact of circadian rhythms on GI function, and the exploration of rare genetic gastrointestinal diseases. Hobbies : I enjoy historical gastronomy and microgreen cultivation. Why I chose Penn : I chose Penn GI because of its innovative approach to gastroenterology, fostering a collaborative environment that encourages both clinical excellence and pioneering research. The access to state-of-the-art facilities, a diverse patient population, and a culture of mentorship and professional development make it the ideal place to advance my career and contribute meaningfully to the field.

medical_school: Perelman School of Medicine at the University of Pennsylvania
name: Sohaib Hashmi, MD, PhD
profile_text_excerpt: Sohaib Hashmi, MD, PhD Medical School: Perelman School of Medicine at the University of Pennsylvania Residency Program: Perelman School of Medicine at the University of Pennsylvania Program/Specialty: Basic Science What inspires me about GI : The gastrointestinal system has diverse functions that impact human health far beyond what people most commonly consider when they think about the gut or liver. The common nature of GI disorders offers so many opportunities to impact the lives of people both clinically and with research. This makes it a hugely exciting field for me. My hobbies : I enjoy cooking, traveling to other parts of the world and trying different cuisines, watching football (soccer), and reading science fiction. Why did I choose Penn : I choose Penn because of the opportunity to serve a diverse patient population, the excellence of clinical medicine that I have seen practiced here through residency, the quality of GI faculty, and the high caliber basic science, translational, and clinical research which are institutional priorities.
residency_program: Perelman School of Medicine at the University of Pennsylvania

medical_school: Dartmouth Medical School
name: Yasmin Kamal, MD, PhD
profile_text_excerpt: Yasmin Kamal, MD, PhD Medical School: Dartmouth Medical School Residency Program: Brigham & Women’s Hospital Program/Specialty: Basic Science What inspires me about GI : The diversity in clinical practice with both procedural and medical management of patients as well as the ability to provide both acute and longitudinal care for patients in both the inpatient and outpatient settings. GI is a very interdisciplinary specialty which requires knowledge of many organ systems, and which intersects with many other subspecialties in medicine thereby allowing for life-long learning in medicine! My interests within GI : Hereditary GI cancer genetic syndromes including Lynch Syndrome, Colorectal cancer, gut-immune dysregulation, PBC/PSC to cholangiocarcinoma transformation, liver transplantation in the setting of malignancy Hobbies : Cooking, Hiking, Travel, National Parks, Arts and Crafts Why I Chose Penn : I chose Penn because of the emphasis and commitment to physician-scientist training within gastroenterology/hepatology as well as the excellent training environment, mentorship, and supportive culture of the program.

name: Gabrielle Aquino, MD
profile_text_excerpt: Gabrielle Aquino, MD Medical School: George Washington University Residency Program: Mount Sinai Hospital Undergraduate: Boston College Email: [REDACTED_EMAIL] Gabby grew up in Rahway, NJ. She went to Boston College for her undergraduate studies and graduated with a major in Biology and a minor in Theology. She worked for a year with the Jesuit Volunteer Corps in Washington, DC then stayed to attend medical school at the George Washington University School of Medicine and Health Sciences. While in DC, she participated in activities that served those experiencing homelessness and volunteered at clinics that provided health care to lower income communities. She completed Internal Medicine Residency at the Mount Sinai Hospital in New York City. Inspired by the mentors and patients she had met along her journey, Gabby decided to pursue an Infectious Diseases fellowship and found a match at Penn. She is exploring new experiences and opportunities but currently has a particular interest in HIV prevention, sexually transmitted infections, and population health. In her free time, Gabby enjoys traveling, watching documentaries, going for long walks, and being at home with her husband and dog.

medical_school: Yale School of Medicine
name: Joseph Ladines Lim, MD
profile_text_excerpt: Joseph Ladines Lim, MD Medical School: Yale School of Medicine Graduate School: Massachusetts Institute of Technology Residency Program: Combined Medicine-Pediatrics at Michigan Medicine, University of Michigan Undergraduate: University of Maryland Email: [REDACTED_EMAIL] Joseph Ladines-Lim was born in Baltimore, MD and obtained his undergraduate degrees at the University of Maryland, his ScD in Chemical Engineering at MIT, and his MD at Yale School of Medicine. He also worked for the Bertha Centre for Social Innovation and Entrepreneurship at the University of Cape Town for a year. During medical school, he took part in projects on Zika exposure and neurodevelopmental sequela in infants in Salvador, Brazil and uptake of tuberculosis preventative therapy in KwaZulu-Natal, South Africa. He completed a combined Medicine-Pediatrics residency at Michigan Medicine (University of Michigan), where he spearheaded projects on antimicrobial stewardship in immigrant and refugee populations in Southeast Michigan, use of ICD-10 codes to estimate rates of inappropriate antibiotic prescribing in large datasets, and firearm safety screening and counseling in primary care. He is now a first-year Infectious Disease fellow at Penn Medicine.
residency_program: Combined Medicine-Pediatrics at Michigan Medicine, University of Michigan

medical_school: Rutgers University
name: Jung Min Seo, MD
profile_text_excerpt: Jung Min Seo, MD Medical School: Rutgers University Residency Program: Baylor College of Medicine Undergraduate: Rutgers University Email: [REDACTED_EMAIL] Jung grew up in New Jersey and attended Rutgers University - New Brunswick for his undergraduate studies where he majored in molecular biology, biochemistry, psychology and minored in chemistry. He attended medical school at Rutgers New Jersey Medical School, then left New Jersey to complete his Internal Medicine residency training at Baylor College of Medicine in Houston, Texas. He completed a chief resident year, where he focused on honing his skills as a clinician educator, then returned to the northeast to start his Infectious Diseases fellowship training at the University of Pennsylvania. His interests include HIV medicine, medical education, general infectious diseases, and HIV prevention. Outside of work, he enjoys traveling to new places, taking care of his plants at home, and baking new recipes.

medical_school: Jefferson University
name: Mary Ferranti, MD
profile_text_excerpt: Mary Ferranti, MD Medical School: Jefferson University Residency Program: Hospital of the University of Pennsylvania Undergraduate: Cornell University Email: [REDACTED_EMAIL] Mary is originally from Syracuse, New York. She attended undergrad at Cornell University majoring in Biology and Society with a focus on the intersection of medicine and the social sciences. She went to medical school at Sidney Kimmel Medical College and was heavily involved in the student-run clinic working with those experiencing homelessness and getting to know the incredible Philadelphia community. She completed Internal Medicine Residency at Penn and was fortunate to meet and work with wonderful mentors in ID in HIV and stewardship. Her current interests in ID focus on HIV prevention and community-based care. In her free time, she enjoys reading, traveling, and spending time with friends - particularly being amateur food critics for Philly's incredible restaurant scene.
residency_program: Hospital of the University of Pennsylvania

medical_school: Universidad Francisco Marroquin
name: Sofia Barrios, MD
profile_text_excerpt: Sofia Barrios, MD Medical School: Universidad Francisco Marroquin Residency Program: Pennsylvania Hospital Undergraduate: Universidad Francisco Marroquin Email: [REDACTED_EMAIL] Sofía was born and raised in Guatemala, where she attended Universidad Francisco Marroquín for undergrad and medical school. She has always been interested in medical education, so after finalizing medical school she served as a teaching assistant in Physiology and worked in curriculum development for the basic sciences. She then moved to Philadelphia in 2021 to complete her internship and residency at Pennsylvania Hospital of the University of Pennsylvania. She decided to stay in Philadelphia to pursue training in Infectious Diseases at the Hospital of the University of Pennsylvania. Her primary interest is in antimicrobial stewardship. In her free time, she enjoys traveling, cooking, and event planning.

medical_school: Perelman School of Medicine at the University of Pennsylvania
name: Zachary M Hostetler, MD
profile_text_excerpt: Zachary M Hostetler, MD Medical School: Perelman School of Medicine at the University of Pennsylvania Residency Program: New York-Presbyterian Hospital/ Weill Cornell Medical Center Undergraduate: Pennsylvania State University Email: [REDACTED_EMAIL] Zachary grew up in the greater Philadelphia area and attended Pennsylvania State University for his undergraduate studies, where he majored in biochemistry and molecular biology and first began his scientific training in a research lab. He then attended the Perelman School of Medicine at the University of Pennsylvania, graduating from the dual degree MD-PhD program after completing dissertation work focused on a bacterial pathway that leads to antibiotic resistance. Following this, Zachary completed his residency training in internal medicine at Weill Cornell Medical Center in New York City. After several years spent living away from the Philadelphia area, Zachary and his family returned to the Philadelphia area so that he could pursue fellowship training in infectious diseases. During his first year of fellowship, Zachary cultivated clinical and research interests in infections caused by antibiotic-resistant Gram-negative bacteria. The current focus of his post-doctoral research revolves around the molecular mechanisms of beta-lactamase enzymes, which are among the most prevalent and most important causes of antibiotic resistance in many clinically important Gram-negative bacteria.
residency_program: New York-Presbyterian Hospital/ Weill Cornell Medical Center

medical_school: Case Western Reserve University
name: Jessica O’Neil, MD
profile_text_excerpt: Jessica O’Neil, MD Medical School: Case Western Reserve University Graduate School: Johns Hopkins University Residency Program: Massachusetts General Hospital Email: [REDACTED_EMAIL] Originally from Cleveland, Ohio, Jess studied Public Health at Johns Hopkins University and then worked at the Maryland Health Services Cost Review Commission before attending Case Western Reserve University School of Medicine. She completed internal medicine residency at Massachusetts General Hospital where she pursued interest in healthcare quality, safety and operations. She moved to Philadelphia with her partner Kyle and cat Meowser. At UPenn , she hopes to explore her interests in antimicrobial stewardship and infection prevention. Outside of work she enjoys biking, kayaking and exploring the Phil ly restaurant scene.

medical_school: Perelman School of Medicine
name: M Elle Saine, MD
profile_text_excerpt: M Elle Saine, MD Medical School: Perelman School of Medicine Graduate School: New York University Residency Program: Hospital of the University of Pennsylvania Undergraduate: Temple University Email: [REDACTED_EMAIL] Elle was born in Colorado and grew up in several states before calling Philadelphia home. She received her BA in Anthropology: Human Biology Track from Temple University and her MA in Skeletal Biology from NYU, where she trained in forensic anthropology and human rights work with the New York Office of the Chief Medical Examiner and The Argentine Forensic Anthropology Team. She then returned to Philadelphia to complete her post-baccalaureate pre-medical training at UPENN, followed by a gap year working as a research analyst in the Center for Epidemiology and Biostatistics (CCEB). She is now a Penn “lifer,” completing her MD-PhD in Epidemiology, Internal Medicine Residency, and now ID Fellowship here. Her research and clinical interests focus on HIV, viral hepatitis, and the intersection of infectious diseases and the opioid epidemic. As an epidemiologist, she investigates the role of disease-related stigma as a barrier in access to healthcare and is interested in developing novel mixed-methods approaches to better understand these experiences. When she is not in the clinic, she enjoys spending time with her cats and finding new and wonderfully weird places to explore.
residency_program: Hospital of the University of Pennsylvania

name: Stephanie Lee, MD
profile_text_excerpt: Stephanie Lee, MD Medical School: University of Oklahoma College of Medicine Residency Program: Med-Peds | University of Miami/Jackson Health Systems Undergraduate: University of Oklahoma Hometown: Oklahoma City, OK Career Interests: Pediatric to adult transition, medical education, ultrasound Anticipated Additional Training During Fellowship: Combined Med-Peds Rheum! What Drew Me to Penn: Penn/CHOP at the time I interviewed had the most amount of active Med-Peds fellows in the country and a large faculty size where you could learn from experts in every area of Rheumatology. I felt with Penn I would be supported to be the Rheumatologist I always aspired to be, and felt very supported in all my career goals when picking Penn! profile_text_excerpt: Jonathan Kotzin, MD, PhD Medical School: Perelman School of Medicine at the University of Pennsylvania Residency Program: Brigham and Women’s Hospital Undergraduate: Stanford University Hometown: Denver, CO Career Interests: Academic rheumatology with an interest in basic mechanisms of immune response dysregulation including the development and function of anti-cytokine autoantibodies. I am also interested in exploring known mechanisms of tumor microenvironment immune suppression as possible novel therapeutics in autoimmunity. What Drew Me to Penn/CHOP: Exceptional clinical faculty, collaborative training environment, world class immunology research, and the city of Philadelphia Hobbies: Spending time outdoors by playing tennis, rowing on the Schuylkill river, hiking sections of the Appalachian trail, or exploring Philly playgrounds and coffee shops with my wife and 1-year-old son.

profile_text_excerpt: JK Vervilles, MD, MPH Medical School: Sidney Kimmel Medical College JK is originally from Bowling Green, KY. Before medical school, he taught middle school science in Minneapolis through Teach For America and the University of Minnesota. At Sidney Kimmel Medical College, he developed interests in Med-Peds primary care, digital health, quality improvement, and public health. When not at the hospital, you can find him with his husband and playing with their three cats, traveling, running along the Schuylkill River, line dancing at Tavern on Camac, or hunkering down in one of Philly’s many cozy coffee shops and reading a good book.

bio_text: Jessica is originally from New Jersey. Prior to medical school, she attended Dartmouth College, where she studied medical anthropology and linguistics. She moved to Philadelphia to attend medical school at Penn, where she pursued her passions for health equity, climate health, and medical education. While at Penn, Jessica has served in national leadership positions, including Associate Director of Medical Students with Disability and Chronic Illness (MSDCI) and as the Perelman School of Medicine’s representative to the Association of American Medical Colleges. Her academic interests include disability health, complex and critical care, pre-hospital and wilderness medicine, and point-of-care ultrasound. Outside of the hospital, she can be found enjoying the outdoors, discovering new pasta shapes, and hanging with her three-legged cat.
medical_school: Dartmouth College
name: Jessica Campanile, MD

bio_text: Leila grew up in Silver Spring, MD. Prior to entering medical school at Johns Hopkins, she studied neuroscience and photojournalism at Northeastern University, worked on oral history projects about the Ethiopian Diaspora, and obtained a Master’s in Public Health in Community-Oriented Primary Care from George Washington University. Blending health, art, and organizing, Leila is passionate about exploring storytelling as a tool for healing and community-building while furthering the wellbeing of marginalized communities both in and out the clinical space. She has special interests in primary care, adolescent health, mental health, and immigrant and refugee health. Outside of the hospital, Leila can be found sitting by water, beating her friends in Uno, and trying to avoid accumulating cavities with her relentless sweet tooth.
name: Leila Habib, MD

bio_text: Vismaya was born and raised in Salt Lake City, where she attended the Spencer Fox Eccles School of Medicine at the University of Utah. As an undergraduate at Harvard, she studied human evolution, which shaped her love of development, genetics, and care across the lifespan. Throughout college and medical school, she led community initiatives connecting folks to well-rounded care, from youth without stable housing in Cambridge to New American patients in South Salt Lake. Her academic interests include interprofessional and community-based care models, improving medical education, and complex congenital disorders. Vismaya also enjoys hiking with her husky, Kaya, reading speculative fiction, and playing board games with friends.
medical_school: the Spencer Fox Eccles School of Medicine
name: Vismaya Kharkar

bio_text: Rachel Lutz is originally from Portland, Oregon where she learned to love nature, fusion food, and expensive coffee. She studied Biochemistry at the University of Oregon, and then attended Yale School of Medicine for medical school. While in medical school she explored research in neuroimmunology and developed a passion for community-based culinary medicine. When she is outside of the hospital, you can find Rachel attending dance classes, enjoying cooking for her friends, and exploring the city (especially the food scene!)
medical_school: Yale School of Medicine
name: Rachel Lutz

bio_text: Rachel grew up in Hamilton, New York. She went to medical school at UCLA, where she also received a PhD in Anthropology. At UCLA she focused on disability research and advocacy both in medical education and in patient care. Outside of medicine, she loves cooking, eating, and spending time with her partner and their two cats.
name: Rachel Parks

bio_text: Though born in New Jersey, Neha's heart belongs to California, where she was raised. Prior to medical school, she studied international studies with a focus on global health inequities.. At UCSF School of Medicine, she was active in advocacy efforts focusing on period poverty and menstrual inequity and worked on capacity-building for LMICs with SaveTheChildren. During medical school, she also earned an MPH in Global Health from Harvard, where she continued her interest in climate change and its impact on marginalized communities in resource limited areas. Outside of school, Neha loves to travel and stay active! You will find her either at the departure area of the airport, or in the sun - finding joy in swimming, hiking, or reading a book.
medical_school: UCSF School of Medicine
name: Neha Pondicherry

bio_text: Nathaniel is a Californian, born and raised. His favorite pastimes are reading, video games, and playing D&D and other card/board games with fiends. He also enjoys hanging out with his wife, Jo Ann, and two dangerously cuddly dogs, Bleu and Brie. Last but not least, he enjoys travel, exploration, and eating great food.
name: Nathaniel Srikureja, MD

bio_text: Rachel was born and raised in the suburbs of Philadelphia. In undergrad, she studied Political Science and Spanish Language at DePauw University. Post-grad, she worked in global health and infectious disease in Atlanta, GA while obtaining her MPH at Emory University. Upon starting medical school at the Uniformed Services University of the Health Sciences she commissioned into the U.S. Public Health Service as an active duty officer with the Indian Health Service (IHS). Following residency, she will provide care to American Indian and Alaska Native communities through her service commitment with the IHS. Rachel's academic interests include global health, infectious disease, underserved medicine, and primary care. In her free time she enjoys baking, sailing, and spending time with her husband Peter, and their two cats, Birdie and Buoy. She is an avid Philadelphia Eagles fan, and looks forward to returning to the City of Brotherly Love for residency.
name: Rachel Burriss, MD

bio_text: Ella is originally from Honolulu, Hawaii but has spent a near decade on the East Coast. She comes to us from Brown University Alpert Medical School where she formed interests in patient-centered communication, medical education, and care of underserved populations. She pursued a dual degree with a Masters in Population Medicine where her research focused on care of individuals with substance use disorders and individuals involved with the criminal legal system. Outside of the hospital, you can find her spending quality time with friends, reading a good book, going for a swim, and enjoying great food.
medical_school: Brown University
name: Ella van Deventer, MD

bio_text: Jane is originally from New Jersey. She attended medical school at Case Western Reserve University in Cleveland, Ohio, where she explored interests in public health, pediatric chronic disease, and community engagement. Her research in clinical informatics led her to pursue an MPH in Quantitative Methods from the Harvard T.H. Chan School of Public Health, where she examined quality outcomes for adolescents and young adults with advanced cancer. Her academic interests include AYA oncology, population health, and advocacy. Outside of the hospital, she loves doing hot yoga, watching early 2000s rom-coms, and cooking any NYT recipe with her partner.
medical_school: medical school at Case Western Reserve University
name: Jane Hinkle, MD

bio_text: Erini comes from a big fat Greek family and is a born and raised New Jersey-ian! She went to undergrad at Johns Hopkins University in Baltimore and stayed there during her gap year. She loved the five years that she called Baltimore home. In her free time, Erini is an avid bookworm, loves to bake, travel, and be outdoors. She is also excited to adopt a cat during residency!
medical_school: undergrad at Johns Hopkins University
name: Erini Papas, MD

bio_text: Klevi was born in a small town in Albania where he spent his first decade before moving to Georgia. Before medical school, he worked with the unhoused in New Haven’s soup kitchens and later as a medical assistant. At Emory School of Medicine, he worked with the Dean of Student Success to found a school-wide tutoring program providing academic guidance to students in need and coordinated the student-run refugee clinic. His interests include improving equity of resources in medical training, immigrant and refugee health, and medical education. When not at the hospital, you can find him trying out new routes at his bouldering gym or cuddling and petting any dog he runs into.
medical_school: Emory School of Medicine
name: Klevi Golloshi, MD

bio_text: Sharon-Rose is originally from Ghana, but also calls Chicago home. She comes to us from the University of Wisconsin School of Medicine and Public Health (UWSMPH) where she was heavily involved in DEI initiatives. Some of these efforts included co-founding the school’s chapter of White Coats for Black Lives (WC4BL), leading a statewide WC4BL rally, and serving as co-president of Wisconsin's chapter of Student National Medical Association (SNMA). Her academic interests include advocacy, medical education reform, health disparity research, and mentoring students who are underrepresented in medicine. Outside of the hospital, Sharon-Rose enjoys catching up with friends and exploring local food spots.
medical_school: the University
name: Sharon-Rose Nartey, MD

bio_text: Spenser was born in Taipei, Taiwan and raised in Taipei and San Jose, California. Prior to medical school, he worked closely with community leaders and volunteered as a community organizer and advocate in a Latinx immigrant community in the Bay Area while also working at a Disability Justice non-profit. At the University of Rochester School of Medicine & Dentistry, he explored his interests in liberation theology, social medicine, and medical education while partnering with community-based organizations in advocacy. His academic interests include transitional care and decolonial praxis in global health. Outside of the hospital, you can find him catching up with friends and perfecting noodle soup recipes.
name: Spenser Chen, MD

bio_text: Victoria is originally from New Jersey but has lived in Philadelphia since starting her undergraduate studies at the University of Pennsylvania. She has been a “Penn lifer” ever since! Prior to medical school she studied English literature, Hispanic Studies, and journalism before joining a post-baccalaureate program in the health sciences. She attended medical school at the Perelman School of Medicine at Penn, where she simultaneously pursued a Master’s in Public Health. Her passions include adolescent medicine, One Health, and global health, particularly partnering with communities in Guatemala and Ecuador. Throughout medical school, she has engaged in public health projects working alongside a community in San Cristóbal, Galápagos. Most recently, she investigated the impacts of climate change on child and adolescent mental health on the island and is working with community partners on interventions to support these young people. She is also passionate about working with adolescents who are experiencing homelessness at the clinic at Covenant House Pennsylvania. She is a dog-lover who is always seeking opportunities to scuba dive, do HIIT workouts, bake chocolate chip cookies, and read fantasy novels!
medical_school: medical school at the Perelman School of Medicine
name: Victoria Moffitt, MD, MPH

bio_text: JK is originally from Bowling Green, KY. Before medical school, he taught middle school science in Minneapolis through Teach For America and the University of Minnesota. After teaching, he spent several years working at Epic, the electronic health records software company, where he helped customize the software for organizations switching from another EHR. At Sidney Kimmel Medical College, he developed interests in Med-Peds primary care, digital health, quality improvement, and public health. When not at the hospital, you can find him with his husband and playing with their three cats, traveling, running along the Schuylkill River, line dancing at Tavern on Camac, or hunkering down in one of Philly’s many cozy coffee shops and reading a good book.
name: JK Vervilles, MD, MPH

bio_text: Kingsley was born in Hong Kong but spent most of his childhood in San Rafael, CA. Prior to medical school, he taught English in South Korea (where he met his husband!), obtained his Master of Science in Epidemiology, and worked in HIV research. At Emory University School of Medicine, he was active in advocacy efforts through the Asian and Pacific Islander American Medical Student Association (APAMSA) and Emory’s LGBTQ+ student group. His academic interests include HIV medicine, transgender health, antimicrobial stewardship, and medical education. When he’s not working, he’s baking, dancing, or cuddling with his dog, Dumpling.
medical_school: Emory University School of Medicine
name: Kingsley Fortin-Leung, MD, MS

bio_text: Lane grew up in Seattle, WA. They went to medical school at UCLA where they helped design and implement a novel Structural Racism and Health Equity curriculum that fosters students’ critical consciousness of racial capitalism, neoliberalism, and abolitionist praxis. They also earned an MPH in Health Policy from Harvard, where they examined the intersections between the medical industrial complex, anti-transgender legislation, and the undermining of intersex bodily autonomy. Their research centers around exploring the sexual health and educational needs of transgender youth and their parents and implementing EHR changes to improve rates of preventative screenings for transgender patients. Their academic interests include medical education and gender-affirming care and they plan to combine clinical practice, health policy advocacy, and community organizing to work toward racial, social, and economic justice for the LGBTQIA2S community. When not at the hospital, you can often find them playing ultimate frisbee, making (and eating!) charcuterie boards, playing extremely time-consuming board games, and trying local restaurants.
name: Lane Kantor, MD, MPH

bio_text: Denysia Allen comes to us from Drexel University College of Medicine, where she enjoyed solving diagnostic mysteries. She spent much of her time mentoring high school students from backgrounds underrepresented in medicine. In addition, she is passionate about advocacy, and has worked with the Philadelphia community, most recently educating families of color on the benefits of receiving the COVID vaccine. She currently plans to pursue a combined hematology-oncology fellowship with focus sickle cell disease, but is still considering other specialties. In her free time, she enjoys spending time with her husband and two dogs, Jade and Joe. One of her favorite past times is exploring the vibrant food scene in Philadelphia and now considers her to be the foodie in the class, always ready to try and new restaurant and offer recommendations to her fellow interns. Originally from Phoenix, she now considers herself a Philly transplant after enjoying it so much and deciding to stay for residency.
medical_school: Drexel University
name: Denysia Allen, MD

bio_text: Roshni comes from New Jersey. She went to medical school in Philly at Temple. While at Temple, she worked at the student-run clinic and developed an interest in improving access to treatment for substance use disorders. Her academic interests include addiction medicine, primary care, and medical education. When not at the hospital, you can find her trying to convince her friends to play one more round of Codenames or Pandemic.
name: Roshni Bhat, MD

bio_text: Okechi is originally from Nigeria, but also calls Nashville home. Prior to medical school, he worked across several countries improving access to medical care and building health infrastructure. At Harvard Medical School and Harvard Kennedy School, he created research and advocacy initiatives including a youth advocacy program to promote the pursuit of health care careers among minority students. His academic interests include health policy and health system development in communities globally. Outside of the hospital, he is often catching up with friends for a pickup basketball game or sketching designs in his notepad.
name: Okechi Boms, MD

bio_text: Natasha is originally from San Rafael, CA outside of San Francisco. Prior to medical school she worked as high school math teacher in San Jose. After teaching she worked in clinical research implementing educational interventions to improve cancer screening. She then went to Dell Medical School in Texas where she was active in mentoring students and in inclusion and belonging initiatives. Her academic interests include humanities in medicine and medical education. Outside the hospital, she loves playing with her dog Max and spending time with her husband who is a Penn internal medicine resident.
medical_school: Dell Medical School
name: Natasha Dass, MD

bio_text: Erica is originally from Dallas, TX. Prior to medical school, she spent a few years working as a behavioral technician providing ABA therapy to kids and adults with Autism. She went to medical school at the University of Texas Medical Branch in Galveston, TX. While at UTMB, she was active in DEI efforts through mentorship and advocacy in Student National Medical Association (SNMA), Pan-African Student Society (PASS), and Colors in Medicine. Long term, she is passionate about transitions of care in pediatrics to adults with complex & special health care needs in the Primary Care setting. When not in the hospital, you can find her recruiting co-residents for kickboxing classes, karaoke, and weekend brunch!
medical_school: medical school at the University
name: Erica Onwuegbuchu, MD

bio_text: Andrea comes from Cleveland, OH where she went to medical school at Case Western Reserve University. While there, she developed a passion for Med-Peds primary care, global health, and public health. She's now part of the Global Health Track and plans to go to Botswana this year with some of her Med-Peds co-residents. When not at work, you can find her checking out a new fitness class, dancing, or going for a bike ride after hitting up one of the class happy hours (two programs = three times the happy hours).
medical_school: medical school at Case Western Reserve University
name: Andrea Szabo, MD

bio_text: Brittany is from Long Island, New York. She attended Duke University for her undergraduate degree and the Ichan School of Medicine at Mount Sinai for her medical degree. She then completed her residency in Internal Medicine-Pediatrics at Penn/CHOP in 2025, and is honored to be serving as Med-Peds chief resident at Penn/CHOP for 2025-2026. She hopes to pursue training in Cardiovascular Medicine to care for adults with congenital heart disease. When she is not spending time at Penn and CHOP, she loves to spend time with her friends and family in Philly and back home in New York. She also likes to exercise, read, travel, watch tennis and explore the Philadelphia restaurant scene.
medical_school: Duke University
name: Brittany Glassberg, MD
